codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly handles all gitignore requirements. The update_gitignore() function creates new files when missing, appends to existing files while preserving content, and correctly detects duplicates. Edge cases (empty files, no trailing newline) are handled properly. The code follows Rust conventions and the project's existing patterns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker-cli skill procedure requires TDD approach (write tests first, see them fail, implement, see them pass). The worker followed this correctly as evidenced by the transcript showing tests were added first and failed before implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'Tests fail as expected (TDD red phase)' before implementation, then 'All tests pass' after implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed the skill's work procedure. All steps executed: read feature, understood code, wrote tests first, ran tests (red phase), implemented feature, ran tests (green phase), built, manually verified, committed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, no deviations reported. Transcript shows each step in order.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature's expectedBehavior mentions 'Ensure .spikes/ directory is not tracked by git' which is implicit in adding to .gitignore, but there's no explicit git status check in the implementation or tests to verify this actually works.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification covers file content checks but not actual git behavior. This is a minor gap - the implementation is correct but not fully verified against real git.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Pre-existing flaky test (test_spike_token_env_override) was noted by worker in discoveredIssues. This is correctly marked as non-blocking and unrelated to this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues includes: 'Pre-existing: test_spike_token_env_override is flaky due to environment variable interference between parallel tests.'

summary: Feature 'cli-init-gitignore' implementation is complete and correct. All requirements met: creates .gitignore if missing, appends to existing, preserves content, detects duplicates. 6 unit tests and 4 integration tests provide comprehensive coverage. No blocking issues. One minor observation: expectedBehavior mentions ensuring .spikes/ is not git-tracked but tests don't verify actual git behavior (only file content).
